text.title: Streaming Response UX Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Streaming Response UX application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Render a live cursor or token indicator whenever a stream is in-flight; never leave the surface frozen with no signal.

rationale.source: Streaming Response UX authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Users abandon a frozen UI within ~3s. Anthropic + Galileo streaming-UX postmortems converge on 'always show progress'.

rule.statement: Expose a cancel control reachable in <=1 click while streaming; cancel must abort upstream request, not only the UI.

rationale.source: Streaming Response UX authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Without server-side cancel, users pay for tokens after they've left the page; SSE / WebSocket clients leak.

rule.statement: Allow the input box to remain editable mid-stream; treat new submit as 'cancel + new request'.

rationale.source: Streaming Response UX authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Locked input causes rage-clicks; Vercel + Replit AI studies show editability is the single biggest perceived-fluidity lift.

rule.statement: Distinguish (a) network drop (b) provider error (c) content-policy block in the UI; never collapse all three to 'something went wrong'.

rationale.source: Streaming Response UX authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Generic error messages stall retries; users misattribute provider errors to their input.

rule.statement: Emit TTFT and abort-rate events to a sink with surface_id, model, p50 / p95 thresholds, and alert rules.

rationale.source: Streaming Response UX authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Without TTFT telemetry the surface degrades silently across deploys; abort-rate predicts churn.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
